objective: Find the strongest case that 'Independent Model Inference Microservice Layer' is duplicative, destabilizing, or wrongly framed.
evidence: C45 already treats inference as a bounded substrate rather than an agent-owned unit.
C36 already owns the lawful externally reachable service boundary, so a new layer risks creating a second ingress plane.
C39 already has sampling and stream semantics; a new protocol family would indicate duplication, not a true missing authority.
T-300 blocks reviving an old-stack layer by implication.
recommended_next_action: Reject the idea in its literal historical form; only consider a narrower redesign inside current authority boundaries.
